utilities.title: Mini-apps

github.cardSubtitle: The utility will run in an isolated iframe. Every call goes through the Host API with permission checks.
github.author: Author: {author}
github.projectOption: Into project — open a specific project first
github.warning: This is code from another person. Reflex applies a CSP that blocks direct network calls, but check the permissions list above — especially the domains under web.fetch and kb.write.

mcp.subtitle: We will connect to the server, read tools/list, generate a UI and server-action proxy.

viaAgent.cardSubtitle: If the repo isn't structured as a Reflex utility, ask the orchestrator to analyse it and generate a wrapper.
viaAgent.footer1: Paste it into any project chat (Sidebar → project → chat). The agent will analyse the repo, design a thin UI on top of its API/CLI, and emit a
viaAgent.promptBody:
Convert the GitHub repo {url} into a Reflex utility.

Steps:
1. Read the repository README (via web.fetch if you have permission, otherwise ask).
2. Identify what it is: npm package / CLI / web app / library.
3. Design a thin Reflex wrapper:
   - If it's an npm package with a JS API → the utility calls it via a server action (actions/run.ts).
   - If it's a CLI → a server action exec's it, with arguments from the UI.
   - If it's a web service → the utility hits its API through reflex.web.fetch (declare the required domain in permissions.web.fetch.domains).
4. UI — one or two forms for the main use-case; default-export functional component.
5. Emit <<reflex:utility>>{...}<</reflex:utility>> with manifest + files.

The manifest must include source.origin = "github:{url}", category = the matching one ("dev", "media", "data", "kb", etc.), and explicit permissions.

Don't invent APIs — if you're unsure about the call shape, ask via <<reflex:question>>.

curated.empty: Catalogue is empty. You can install from GitHub manually (at the bottom of the page).
curated.needSpace: Create a space first (the /onboarding wizard) — utilities are tied to a project.

manifest.editPlaceholder: What to change? E.g. add dark mode, cache results in data/cache.json, tighten input validation…

helper.title: Assistant
helper.welcome: Ask something about this mini-app's data. The dialog is saved as a normal conversation — you can open it in full via the icon in the corner.
helper.projectOnly: Chat is only available in project-scope utilities.

button.title: Create public link
button.label: Share
button.footer: The link will only work once the ngrok tunnel is started (Settings → Public links).
button.createdNoTunnel: Link created; start the ngrok tunnel in settings to expose it externally

project.noPublicWidgets: There are no public widgets on the dashboard.
project.footer: Widgets are serialized at the time the link is created. Auto-refresh and interactivity are only available in Reflex.

images.saveToKb: Save as a separate KB record (kind: image)
images.takesAMinute: May take up to a minute
images.noKeys: Need an Unsplash / Pexels key in Settings → Images.
